paper_title: A Pilot Study on the Degree of Tooth Staining Caused by Pollen Water and Chlorhexidine: In Vitro Study

methods.description: Preparation of three solutions for testing: Chlorhexidine as a positive control, pollen water as the test group, and distilled water as a negative control.

steps.description: Chlorhexidine 0.12% licensed for use as a mouthwash. (positive control)
steps.searchable_summary: prepare Chlorhexidine solution for use as a positive control

steps.description: Pollen water (date palm pollen water suspension) obtained commercially from a manufacturer, licensed for internal/external use (100% pure, cold-pressed). (test group)
steps.searchable_summary: obtain and prepare pollen water for use as a test group
prepare distilled water for use as a negative control

methods.description: Preparation of extracted human molar teeth for testing, ensuring they meet specific criteria and are cleaned and sized appropriately.

steps.description: 24 extracted intact human molar teeth were used in this study. Inclusion criteria were: caries-free teeth with no cracks, no previous root canal therapy, and no crowns, which were recently extracted for orthodontic treatment purposes.
steps.searchable_summary: select and prepare human molar teeth based on inclusion criteria

steps.description: The teeth surfaces were cleaned using a scaler to dislodge debris and periodontal instruments to remove calculus. This was followed by brushing using a rubber cup.
steps.searchable_summary: clean teeth surfaces using scaler and periodontal instruments

steps.description: Each tooth was decoronated, and the crown was prepared to be at a size of 8 mm ×11 mm × 2.5 mm using a diamond bur.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare tooth crowns to specified size using diamond bur

methods.description: Measurement of color changes in prepared tooth samples using a digital imaging spectrophotometer.

steps.description: Each prepared sample was placed onto the chamber of a benchtop digital imaging spectrophotometer, which was previously calibrated according to the manufacturer's specifications.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate and use spectrophotometer to measure color of samples

steps.description: Color measurements were obtained in triplicate, and the mean value was considered as the final measurement.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain and average triplicate color measurements

methods.description: Procedure for soaking tooth specimens in different solutions and measuring staining over a period of 6 weeks.

steps.description: Each specimen was soaked in Chlorhexidine, pollen water, or normal water, taking in mind changing the solutions every day, and staining was measured weekly for 6 weeks.
steps.searchable_summary: soak specimens in solutions and measure staining weekly

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the color change data, including tests for normality and variance analysis.

steps.description: The color change (∆E) was calculated using the following Eq. (1): Where: ∆ represents the difference between the object being measured and its reference L* represents lightness a* and b* are the chromaticity coordinates.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate color change using chromaticity coordinates

steps.description: Data normality was checked using the Shapiro-Wilk test with p < 0.05, indicating non-normally distributed data.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Shapiro-Wilk test for data normality

steps.description: The color differences for L, a, b, C*, and H* were calculated and analyzed using the Friedman test (non-parametric method of repeated-measure analysis of variance (ANOVA)).
steps.searchable_summary: perform Friedman test for color differences

steps.description: Repeated-measure ANOVA was applied separately for each solution.
steps.searchable_summary: apply repeated-measure ANOVA for each solution

steps.description: For significant findings, the paired sample t-test was performed to assess for individual significant results.
steps.searchable_summary: perform paired sample t-test for significant findings
